topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification pinged, a small, insistent sound against the muted hum of the television. A profile picture loaded: a familiar, slightly sneering face that instantly tightened a knot in my stomach. It was Mark. Mark, the architect of my awkward middle school years, the one whose shadow I’d spent the better part of a decade trying to outrun. I blinked slowly, the effort feeling monumental. Why was this happening now? I barely moved from the sofa, letting the inertia of the cushions hold me captive. I glanced at the screen, a dull ache spreading across my forehead. Accepting felt like lifting a boulder. I let the message sit there, an unopened invitation to a history I was thoroughly uninterested in revisiting.

[story 2]

My phone vibrated on the nightstand, demanding attention. It was a request from Mark. I let my hand drift towards it, then let it fall back onto the worn fabric of the duvet. The sheer effort of engaging with anything felt excessive. I stared at the ceiling, tracing the cracks in the plaster with my gaze. A familiar wave of sluggishness washed over me, heavy and warm. I considered deleting the notification, burying it under layers of unread messages. But then I saw the name. A slow, almost imperceptible sigh escaped my lips. Maybe it wasn't worth the energy expenditure. I decided, with the ease of someone already defeated, to leave it pending.

[story 3]

The screen lit up with a new connection request. My shoulders slumped further, a physical manifestation of the low energy currently saturating my system. It was him. That familiar, irritating presence from my youth. I didn't bother to sit up. My head rested against the pillow, the world outside the window seeming impossibly distant and demanding. A strange sense of inertia settled over me; the urge to simply let things drift by became overwhelming. I watched the little avatar pop up, a faint flicker of something—maybe mild curiosity, maybe just boredom—crossing my features before I decided, without even truly thinking, to ignore the summons.
